components.role: Inverting amplifier — gain set by -Rf/Ri, signal phase is inverted
Feedback resistor — sets gain magnitude as Av = Rf/Ri
Input resistor — sets gain and input impedance (Zin = Ri)
Input coupling capacitor — blocks DC, forms highpass with Ri
Output coupling capacitor — blocks DC bias
Bias divider upper — Vcc/2 reference for non-inverting input
Bias divider lower — Vcc/2 reference for non-inverting input

dsp_model.description: Inverted gain with coupling-cap highpass; input impedance equals Ri

behavioral_description: A clean, phase-inverting gain stage commonly used in overdrive and distortion circuits where diodes are placed in the feedback loop. The inverting topology has a key advantage: its input impedance is set by Ri (the virtual ground summing point), which is well-defined and independent of the op-amp. This makes it ideal for summing multiple signals or for cascading with predictable loading. The gain is -Rf/Ri, providing precise control. In pedal circuits, this topology is the workhorse of the Tube Screamer, RAT, and countless other designs — the inverting input is where the magic happens when clipping diodes are added to the feedback path.
